name: Factory Droid
description: Factory's agent-native CLI agent ("Droid") for autonomous, multi-model software development; enterprise-positioned.

first-release.comment: Droids were publicly launched Sep 25, 2025 alongside the Series B; the Factory platform existed earlier in beta but the Droid agents GA dates to this announcement.

status.comment: Actively developed and commercially sold (Pro/Plus/Max/Teams/Enterprise plans) as of June 2026.

license.comment: The public Factory-AI/factory repo is docs/issues only and carries an 'All rights reserved' notice; the Droid CLI source is not openly licensed.

specialization.comment: Broad coding/engineering agent (feature dev, refactors, bug fixes, incident triage); enterprise-positioned but not niche-specialized.

interface.comment: Primary surface is the terminal CLI (Droid); also ships VS Code/JetBrains/Zed extensions and a Web app, plus Slack/Linear/Jira delegation surfaces.

host-ides.comment: CLI runs in the terminal; extensions for VS Code, JetBrains, and Zed; Web app runs in the browser. Zed has no dedicated tag here.

open-source.comment: Droid CLI is proprietary; the public repo holds docs/issues with 'All rights reserved'.

self-contained-vs-plugin.comment: Core product is a standalone CLI tool; also distributed as IDE extensions and backed by a cloud service (cloud agents, Droid Computers, Web app).

default-model-provider.comment: Supports leading frontier and open-weight models (GPT-5, Claude Opus/Sonnet, Gemini, etc.); no single default provider — model-agnostic by design.

model-choice.comment: A /model command switches models; per-droid YAML frontmatter sets model selection.

bring-your-own-key.comment: BYOK supported via config.json / custom: model prefixes; pricing page references a BYOK allowance per plan.

local-model-support.comment: BYOK custom model endpoints are documented but the docs make no explicit mention of local runtimes (Ollama/LM Studio/vLLM); local support could not be confirmed (checked 2026-06-05).

multi-file-editing.comment: Handles feature development, refactors, and multi-step missions that edit across a codebase.

autonomous-task-execution.comment: Auto mode runs work to a configurable autonomy level (low/medium/high, plus skip-permissions) chaining edit/run/fix; Droid Exec headless mode runs full tasks non-interactively in CI.

terminal-command-execution.comment: Execute tool runs shell commands with per-command risk levels gated by the autonomy setting.

mcp-support.comment: Ships /mcp command and mcp.json config; per-droid tool policies can expose specific MCP servers.

subagents-parallel-agents.comment: Custom Droids are subagents invoked via a Task tool with subagent_type; missions can run multiple worker agents in parallel.

codebase-indexing.comment: Droid emphasizes persistent context/knowledge across surfaces, but primary docs do not confirm a built semantic embeddings index of the repo (vs ad-hoc file reads); could not be verified (checked 2026-06-05).

planning-mode.comment: Spec Mode is an explicit read-only planning phase; approved specs are saved as markdown in .factory/docs/ before implementation.

multimodal-input.comment: No primary-source confirmation that the CLI accepts image/screenshot input; the underlying models are vision-capable but Droid image input could not be verified (checked 2026-06-05).

browser-computer-use.comment: No documented capability to drive a real browser/GUI to verify changes; 'Droid Computers' are remote cloud dev environments, not browser-driving agents.

operating-mode.comment: Interactive terminal chat; supervised at lower autonomy (ask-approval); fully autonomous at higher autonomy / Droid Exec; async via delegated missions (Slack/Linear/Jira) and cloud agents. No inline completion.

inline-completion.comment: Terminal/CLI agent with no as-you-type tab autocomplete.

background-async-execution.comment: Tasks can be delegated (Slack/Linear/Jira) and run on cloud agents/Droid Computers while the developer does other work; missions resume across sessions.

persistent-memory.comment: Persistent sessions/missions retain transcripts, tool calls and context; Droids keep knowledge across surfaces and sessions.

checkpoints-rollback.comment: No documented built-in checkpoint/undo to revert an agent run as a unit; git-based recovery is implied but a dedicated snapshot/rollback feature could not be confirmed (checked 2026-06-05).

git-integration.comment: Droid makes git operations conversational (diffs, commits) and integrates with PR workflows via Droid Action.

pr-creation.comment: Droid Action runs on pull requests and @droid can generate PR descriptions; GitHub integration supports PR creation/updating.

custom-rules-instructions.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md as the primary project context file (Claude Code CLAUDE.md equivalent).

hooks-extensibility.comment: Supports Skills, MCP, hooks, and plugins; plugins bundle skills/commands/agents/MCP servers into installable packages.

permission-model.comment: Configurable autonomy levels (off/low/medium/high/skip-permissions) decide when commands auto-run vs ask; denylist and sandbox checks force approval for dangerous patterns.

self-host-on-prem.comment: Enterprise plan offers on-premise deployment and dedicated compute.

privacy-mode.comment: Teams plan offers zero data retention options; Enterprise adds full admin/compliance controls.

enterprise-controls.comment: Teams: SSO with SAML/SCIM provisioning; Enterprise: audit logging, on-premise/dedicated compute, full admin controls (RBAC and VPC/on-prem isolation).

pricing-model.comment: Subscription tiers (Pro/Plus/Max) with included usage; Extra Usage prepaid credits ($10 min) for pay-as-you-go; BYOK allowance per plan. No genuine free tier (no free plan listed).

free-tier.comment: Pricing page lists no standing free plan; entry is the $20/mo Pro plan (checked 2026-06-05).

entry-price-usd-mo.comment: Pro plan at $20/month is the cheapest paid plan (checked 2026-06-05).

maturity.comment: GA since Sep 2025, well-funded (Series B/C), enterprise contracts, polished multi-surface product and docs — solid but still rapidly evolving (4/5, not 5).

community-size.comment: Active growing community (Discord, third-party skill/plugin repos) but smaller than Cursor/Copilot/Claude Code scale; single-vendor commercial tool (estimate, checked 2026-06-05).

extension-plugin-ecosystem.comment: Official plugin marketplace plus growing third-party skill/plugin/subagent repos and MCP server support.

project-memory-files.comment: Auto-loads AGENTS.md as the standard project context file ingested at the start of each conversation.

memory-scopes.comment: Config/droids/skills can be defined at user scope (~/.factory/) and project scope (<project>/.factory/). Enterprise team settings exist via Teams plan but folder/enterprise rule scoping is not clearly documented.

scoped-rules-files.comment: AGENTS.md is an always-on context file; skills are pulled in on demand (agent-requested). No documented path-glob or file-type-based conditional rule activation.

auto-memory.comment: Context persists across sessions but no documented mechanism for the agent to autonomously write cross-session memories (manual AGENTS.md only); could not confirm any auto-memory feature (checked 2026-06-05).

skill-support.comment: Claude Code-compatible .factory/skills system (/skills command); packaged reusable capabilities loaded on demand.

skill-invocation.comment: Skills are model-invoked automatically based on the task; plugin commands are user-invoked via /command-name slash commands.

always-on-surface.comment: Claude Code-compatible skills use progressive disclosure — name/description available for the model to select, full skill body loaded only on invocation.

skill-distribution.comment: Official plugin marketplace plus git-hosted marketplaces (droid plugin marketplace add <git-url>); skills can also be checked into .factory/skills as manual files.
